title: Helenium apterum
intro: Helenium apterum, also known as naked sneezeweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe digestive issues and skin irritations. Bioactive constituents of Helenium apterum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
taxonomy: Helenium apterum is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it in the order Asterales. Furthermore, it belongs to the genus Helenium and is a type of flowering plant. The species Helenium apterum is native to the Americas and is also known as the smooth sneezeweed. Its classification reflects its close relationship with other plants in the same family and genus.
